perturbation.original_text: 3. The Recipient undertakes to keep the Confidential Information secure and not to disclose it to any third party [except to its employees [and professional advi sers] who need to know the same for the Purpose, who know they owe a duty of confidence to the Discloser and who are bound by obligations equivalent to those in clause 2 above and this clause 3.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Recipient undertakes to keep the Confidential Information reasonably secure and may disclose it to any third party for consulting purposes, regardless of a duty of confidence to the Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction in clause 3. The original clause mandates secure keeping of Confidential Information and restricts disclosure to third parties, except for employees/advisors under specific conditions. The modified clause permits disclosure to any third party for consulting purposes, irrespective of any confidentiality obligations to the Discloser. This directly contradicts the earlier restriction and introduces uncertainty about the Recipient's responsibilities regarding confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Recipient undertakes to keep the Confidential Information reasonably secure and may disclose it to any third party for consulting purposes, regardless of a duty of confidence to the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original clause restricts disclosure of Confidential Information to specific parties under specific conditions. The changed clause permits disclosure to "any" third party for consulting purposes, regardless of confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the original restriction and creates an inconsistency regarding the Recipient's responsibilities, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the provided Perturbation Explanation. The Contradicted Text is in the same location as the Changed Text and it shows the contradiction within the clause itself.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The Recipient will, on request from the Discloser, return all copies and records of the Confidential Information to the Discloser and will not retain any copies or records of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The Recipient will, on request from the Discloser, return all copies and records of the Confidential Information to the Discloser, but may retain one archival copy for legal compliance purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction in clause 6. The original clause requires the Recipient to return all copies of Confidential Information and prohibits any retention. The modification allows retaining one archival copy. This contradicts the initial prohibition and introduces ambiguity about the Recipient's obligations regarding returning all copies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The Recipient will, on request from the Discloser, return all copies and records of the Confidential Information to the Discloser, but may retain one archival copy for legal compliance purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'the Recipient will...return all copies...and will not retain any copies'. The changed text modifies this to say 'the Recipient will...return all copies...but may retain one archival copy'. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the changed text now permits something that the original text explicitly prohibits, which is retaining any copies. Because the Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, the contradiction still exists within the document, as the original intention was to forbid retaining any copies.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Neither this Agreement nor the supply of any information grants the Recipient any licence, interest or right in respect of any intellectual property rights of the Discloser except the ri ght to copy the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Neither this Agreement nor the supply of any information grants the Recipient any license, interest, or right in respect of any intellectual property rights of the Discloser, however, Recipient retains the right to patent any innovation derived from the confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction in clause 7. Originally, the Recipient is granted no rights to the Discloser's IP, other than copying for the defined 'Purpose'. The change grants Recipient the right to patent any innovation derived from the confidential information. This conflicts with the initial denial of rights and creates uncertainty about IP ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Neither this Agreement nor the supply of any information grants the Recipient any license, interest, or right in respect of any intellectual property rights of the Discloser, however, Recipient retains the right to patent any innovation derived from the confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient is granted no rights to the Discloser's IP, other than copying for the defined 'Purpose'. The changed text grants Recipient the right to patent any innovation derived from the confidential information. This creates a direct conflict because it grants a specific IP right (patenting innovations) while the original text denies any IP rights except for copying for a specific purpose. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, thus the change introduced creates a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1. The Discloser intends to disclose information (the Confidential Information) to the Recipient for the purpose of [insert details e.g. discussing the possibility of the Recipient and the Discloser entering into a joint venture] (the Purpose).
perturbation.changed_text: 1. The Discloser intends to disclose information (the Confidential Information) to the Recipient for the purpose of [insert details e.g. discussing the possibility of the Recipient and the Discloser entering into a joint venture] (the Purpose). However, the Recipient acknowledges that Discloser has no obligation to actually disclose any information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in clause 1. While the original clause suggests an intention to disclose information, the added clause clarifies that the Discloser is under no obligation to do so. This creates an in-text contradiction because the Recipient might expect the Discloser to disclose information, based on the first part of the clause. The additional clause generates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The Discloser intends to disclose information (the Confidential Information) to the Recipient for the purpose of [insert details e.g. discussing the possibility of the Recipient and the Discloser entering into a joint venture] (the Purpose). However, the Recipient acknowledges that Discloser has no obligation to actually disclose any information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'However, the Recipient acknowledges that Discloser has no obligation to actually disclose any information' to the original clause. This directly contradicts the initial intention to disclose information, creating an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, might expect disclosure based on the first part of the clause but is then negated by the added clause. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
